channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump threatens retaliatory 200% tariff on European wine after EU proposes American whiskey tariff
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened a 200% tariff on European wine, champagne and spirits if the European Union goes forward with a planned tariff on American whiskey. The European tariff, which was unveiled in response to steel and aluminum tariffs by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pakistan's leader meets survivors of deadly train hijack and the commandos who ended the siege
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif was in the restive southwestern Balochistan province on Thursday to meet survivors of a train attack and the commandos who rescued over 300 passengers from insurgents who killed 21 civilian and four troops.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming in from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia says it has retaken Kursk's biggest town as Putin advisor says truce would help Ukraine
item.description: Russia claimed Thursday that its troops have driven the Ukrainian army out of the biggest town in Russia's Kursk border region, as a senior Kremlin official said that a U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steve Witkoff has gone from spotting Trump cash at a deli to helping him navigate Ukraine and Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russia news agencies reported that Trump special envoy Steve Witkoff arrived in Moscow on Thursday for high-level talks aimed at ending Russia's invasion of Ukraine, the latest high-profile assignment for the real estate magnate turned White House foreign policy fixer.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Study says endangered Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Belgian prosecutors make arrests in a corruption probe linked to the European Parliament
item.description: Belgian federal prosecutors say they've arrested several individuals as part of a corruption probe linked to the European Parliament amid reports in local media that Chinese company Huawei bribed EU lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump set to meet with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Naples struck by 4.4-magnitude quake causing minor damage and light injuries
item.description: ROME (AP) — The southern Italian city of Naples was struck by a 4.4-magnitude quake early Thursday that caused only minor damage and sent 11 people to the hospital, the most serious suffering contusions after part of a ceiling collapsed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Africa-EU summit centers on boosting trade and diplomatic ties as both feel Trump's impact
item.description: Senior European Union officials are in South Africa for a summit with President Cyril Ramaphosa that centers on bolstering trade and diplomatic ties as both feel the impact of the Trump administration’s confrontational foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis is stable and showing improvements but will remain hospitalized
item.description: Pope Francis' health has taken a turn for the better this week as he continues to show improvement following a Monday update that listed him as “out of danger from death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Duda calls on US to place nuclear weapons in Poland
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's President Andrzej Duda again called on the United States to deploy nuclear weapons to Poland as a deterrent to Russia, the latest indication that the frontline NATO nation is increasingly considering nuclear protection as fears of Russia grow.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia says it has retaken Kursk's biggest town as Putin advisor says truce would help Ukraine
item.description: Russia claimed Thursday that its troops have driven the Ukrainian army out of the biggest town in Russia’s Kursk border region, as a senior Kremlin official said that a U.S.-proposed 30-day ceasefire in the three-year war on Ukraine would help Kyiv by giving its military a break.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: They crossed the world to reach the US. Now deported under Trump, they're stuck in Panama
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — They crossed oceans to get to the U.S., fleeing conflict, religious persecution, poverty and government crackdowns in countries such as Afghanistan, Somalia, Cameroon, China, Pakistan and Iran.
item.dc:creator: MATÍAS DELACROIX and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump envoys begin Russia outreach after Ukraine breakthrough
item.description: Trump administration officials are reaching out to their Russian counterparts as the White House says the ball is in Moscow’s court to agree to a ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: US puts Putin in hot seat with Ukraine ceasefire proposal
item.description: The United States has for the first time shifted the pressure to Russia in President Trump’s push for a ceasefire in Ukraine, after securing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s support for a 30-day truce in the war.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate confronts Trump border czar over ICE arrest of Columbia graduate
item.description: New York State Assembly member and mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani confronted President Trump's border czar, Tom Homan, in Albany on Wednesday over the recent arrest of Columbia University graduate and green card holder Mahmoud Khalil by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Search for Sudiksha Konanki, missing Pitt student, continues in Dominican Republic
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki was last seen early Thursday morning on a beach near Punta Cana. Police have spoken with a man allegedly with her at the time.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's conservatives trying to associate Carney with Trudeau
item.description: Conservatives are on the defense after the Liberal Party picked Carney as Canada's next leader, a journalist says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says mineral deal ‘not main topic on agenda’ in Ukraine meeting
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio and a U.S. delegation will meet with Ukrainian leaders on Tuesday in Saudi Arabia to discuss ways to end the war between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ex-Philippine President Duterte shrugs off possible arrest by ICC for drug war during trip to Hong Kong
item.description: Ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte visited Hong Kong on Sunday, leading to speculation that he may be attempting to evade a possible arrest warrant by the ICC.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope thanks volunteers for 'miracle of tenderness' as another Holy Year event passes without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis thanked volunteers Sunday for the “miracle of tenderness” they offer the sick, as he continued his treatment for double pneumonia and doctors reported some positive news.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Romania blocks frontrunner from postponed presidential race
item.description: Protests have erupted in Romania after authorities barred Calin Georgescu, a candidate critical of NATO, from running in May's presidential election re-run.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader that has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: 12 people injured by 3 men shooting randomly at Toronto pub customers
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A dozen people were injured in a shooting at an eastern Toronto pub in what police called a reckless act of violence by three men who entered the bar and fired randomly without warning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Unexploded WWII bomb in Paris halts Eurostar travel to London, Brussels
item.description: Eurostar announced the cancellation of all its high-speed trains connecting Paris to London and Brussels on Friday after the discovery of a World War-II era bomb near the tracks serving Eurostar’s hub in the French capital, Gare du Nord.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs not the way Trump should slow fentanyl flow: Bolton
item.description: "American importers pay the tariffs and then do everything they can to pass that cost on to American consumers," John Bolton said.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation
